req.title: Timezone handling
req.statement: Timestamps stored shall be UTC; UI shall render in user's configured timezone.
error_pathways: default to UTC when tz missing

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for UTC Storage and Timezone Rendering

## Project Structure Assumption
```
project/
├── models/
│   └── event.py          # Data models with timestamp fields
├── services/
│   └── timezone_service.py  # Timezone conversion logic
├── api/
│   └── endpoints.py      # API layer
└── ui/
    └── formatters.py     # UI rendering/formatting
```

---

## Test File

```python
"""
Tests for Requirement: Timestamps stored shall be UTC;
UI shall render in user's configured timezone.

Coverage areas:
1. Storage layer - timestamps are persisted as UTC
2. Ingestion layer - incoming timestamps are normalized to UTC
3. Conversion service - accurate timezone conversion
4. UI formatter - renders in user's configured timezone
5. Edge cases - DST transitions, offsets, invalid zones
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo  # Python 3.9+; use pytz for earlier versions


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def utc_now():
    """A timezone-aware UTC datetime for use across tests."""
    return datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


@pytest.fixture
def naive_utc_now():
    """A naive datetime that represents UTC (no tzinfo attached)."""
    return datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 0)


@pytest.fixture
def user_eastern():
    """Simulated user profile configured for US/Eastern."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-001"
    user.timezone = "America/New_York"
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def user_tokyo():
    """Simulated user profile configured for Asia/Tokyo."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-002"
    user.timezone = "Asia/Tokyo"
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def user_berlin():
    """Simulated user profile configured for Europe/Berlin."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-003"
    user.timezone = "Europe/Berlin"
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def user_no_timezone():
    """Simulated user with no configured timezone."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-004"
    user.timezone = None
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def mock_db_session():
    """Mock database session for storage assertions."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def sample_event(utc_now):
    """A domain event with a UTC timestamp."""
    from models.event import Event
    return Event(id="evt-001", name="Test Event", created_at=utc_now)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Storage Layer – Timestamps Must Be Persisted as UTC
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestTimestampStoredAsUTC:

    def test_event_created_at_is_stored_with_utc_tzinfo(self, sample_event):
        """Timestamp on a newly created record carries UTC tzinfo."""
        assert sample_event.created_at.tzinfo is not None, (
            "created_at must be timezone-aware"
        )
        assert sample_event.created_at.utcoffset() == timedelta(0), (
            "created_at must have zero UTC offset (i.e., be UTC)"
        )

    def test_event_updated_at_is_stored_as_utc(self):
        """updated_at timestamp reflects UTC when the model is saved."""
        from models.event import Event
        event = Event(id="evt-002", name="Updated Event")
        event.touch()  # simulates an update

        assert event.updated_at.tzinfo == timezone.utc, (
            "updated_at must be UTC after touch()"
        )

    def test_model_factory_always_uses_utc(self):
        """Model factory method never produces a naive timestamp."""
        from models.event import Event
        event = Event.create(name="Factory Event")

        assert event.created_at.tzinfo is not None, (
            "Factory-created events must have timezone-aware timestamps"
        )
        assert event.created_at.utcoffset() == timedelta(0)

    def test_timestamp_is_not_naive(self, naive_utc_now):
        """Naive datetimes are rejected or normalized before storage."""
        from models.event import Event
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, TypeError)):
            # Model must refuse a naive datetime
            Event(id="evt-003", name="Bad Event", created_at=naive_utc_now)

    def test_non_utc_timestamp_is_rejected_on_assignment(self):
        """Assigning a non-UTC aware timestamp raises an error."""
        from models.event import Event
        eastern_time = datetime(
            2024, 6, 15, 10, 30, 0,
            tzinfo=ZoneInfo("America/New_York")
        )
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, TypeError)):
            Event(id="evt-004", name="TZ Event", created_at=eastern_time)

    def test_database_write_stores_utc_value(self, mock_db_session, utc_now):
        """The value written to the database has UTC offset."""
        from models.event import Event
        event = Event(id="evt-005", name="DB Event", created_at=utc_now)

        event.save(session=mock_db_session)

        call_kwargs = mock_db_session.add.call_args
        persisted_event = call_kwargs[0][0]
        assert persisted_event.created_at.utcoffset() == timedelta(0), (
            "Value committed to DB must be UTC"
        )

    def test_bulk_insert_all_timestamps_are_utc(self, mock_db_session):
        """Bulk-inserted records all carry UTC timestamps."""
        from models.event import Event
        events = [
            Event.create(name=f"Bulk Event {i}") for i in range(10)
        ]
        for event in events:
            assert event.created_at.tzinfo == timezone.utc, (
                f"Event '{event.name}' does not have a UTC timestamp"
            )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Ingestion – Incoming Timestamps Are Normalized to UTC
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestIncomingTimestampNormalization:

    def test_api_payload_with_offset_is_normalized_to_utc(self):
        """Timestamps submitted with +05:30 offset are converted to UTC."""
        from services.timezone_service import normalize_to_utc
        ist_time = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 20, 0, 0,
                            tzinfo=ZoneInfo("Asia/Kolkata"))
        result = normalize_to_utc(ist_time)

        expected_utc = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        assert result == expected_utc
        assert result.tzinfo == timezone.utc

    def test_api_payload_with_negative_offset_is_normalized(self):
        """Timestamps with -05:00 (EST) offset are stored as UTC."""
        from services.timezone_service import normalize_to_utc
        est_time = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 9, 30, 0,
                            tzinfo=ZoneInfo("America/New_York"))
        result = normalize_to_utc(est_time)

        # EST is UTC-4 in summer (EDT), so 9:30 EDT → 13:30 UTC
        assert result.tzinfo == timezone.utc
        assert result.utcoffset() == timedelta(0)

    def test_naive_timestamp_raises_error_during_normalization(self):
        """Naive timestamps are not accepted — caller must specify zone."""
        from services.timezone_service import normalize_to_utc
        naive = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 0)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="timezone-aware"):
            normalize_to_utc(naive)

    def test_already_utc_timestamp_is_unchanged(self, utc_now):
        """Normalizing an already-UTC timestamp is a no-op."""
        from services.timezone_service import normalize_to_utc
        result = normalize_to_utc(utc_now)
        assert result == utc_now
        assert result.tzinfo == timezone.utc

    def test_iso8601_string_with_offset_is_parsed_as_utc(self):
        """ISO 8601 strings with offsets are parsed and stored as UTC."""
        from services.timezone_service import parse_to_utc
        iso_string = "2024-06-15T10:30:00-04:00"
        result = parse_to_utc(iso_string)

        expected = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        assert result == expected

    def test_iso8601_string_without_offset_raises_error(self):
        """ISO 8601 strings lacking offset info are rejected."""
        from services.timezone_service import parse_to_utc
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            parse_to_utc("2024-06-15T14:30:00")  # no offset


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Timezone Conversion Service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestTimezoneConversionService:

    def test_convert_utc_to_eastern_summer(self, utc_now):
        """UTC 14:30 converts to Eastern Daylight Time (UTC-4) = 10:30."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        result = convert_to_timezone(utc_now, "America/New_York")

        assert result.hour == 10
        assert result.minute == 30
        assert str(result.tzinfo) == "America/New_York"

    def test_convert_utc_to_eastern_winter(self):
        """UTC 14:30 in January converts to Eastern Standard Time (UTC-5) = 09:30."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        winter_utc = datetime(2024, 1, 15, 14, 30, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        result = convert_to_timezone(winter_utc, "America/New_York")

        assert result.hour == 9
        assert result.minute == 30

    def test_convert_utc_to_tokyo(self, utc_now):
        """UTC 14:30 converts to Asia/Tokyo (UTC+9) = 23:30."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        result = convert_to_timezone(utc_now, "Asia/Tokyo")

        assert result.hour == 23
        assert result.minute == 30

    def test_convert_utc_to_tokyo_next_day(self):
        """UTC 20:00 converts to Asia/Tokyo next calendar day."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        late_utc = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 20, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        result = convert_to_timezone(late_utc, "Asia/Tokyo")

        assert result.day == 16  # next day
        assert result.hour == 5

    def test_convert_utc_to_utc_is_identity(self, utc_now):
        """Converting UTC to UTC returns the same moment."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        result = convert_to_timezone(utc_now, "UTC")

        assert result == utc_now

    def test_invalid_timezone_raises_error(self, utc_now):
        """An unrecognized timezone string raises an appropriate error."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, KeyError), match="timezone"):
            convert_to_timezone(utc_now, "Mars/OlympusMons")

    def test_conversion_preserves_utc_equivalence(self, utc_now):
        """Converted timestamp represents the same instant as UTC source."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        result = convert_to_timezone(utc_now, "America/Chicago")

        # Both must represent the same moment in time
        assert result.astimezone(timezone.utc) == utc_now

    def test_all_common_timezones_convert_without_error(self, utc_now):
        """Conversion succeeds for a representative set of timezones."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        timezones = [
            "America/New_York", "America/Chicago", "America/Denver",
            "America/Los_Angeles", "Europe/London", "Europe/Berlin",
            "Europe/Paris", "Asia/Tokyo", "Asia/Shanghai", "Asia/Kolkata",
            "Australia/Sydney", "Pacific/Auckland",
        ]
        for tz in timezones:
            result = convert_to_timezone(utc_now, tz)
            assert result is not None, f"Conversion failed for {tz}"
            assert result.astimezone(timezone.utc) == utc_now, (
                f"UTC equivalence broken for {tz}"
            )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. DST (Daylight Saving Time) Edge Cases
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestDSTEdgeCases:

    def test_dst_spring_forward_gap(self):
        """Timestamps in the DST spring-forward gap are handled gracefully."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        # 2024-03-10 07:00 UTC = 02:00 EST which springs forward to 03:00 EDT
        spring_forward_utc = datetime(2024, 3, 10, 7, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        result = convert_to_timezone(spring_forward_utc, "America/New_York")

        assert result is not None
        # The clock jumps, but UTC is unambiguous
        assert result.astimezone(timezone.utc) == spring_forward_utc

    def test_dst_fall_back_ambiguous_time(self):
        """Ambiguous fall-back times are resolved without data loss."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        # 2024-11-03 06:00 UTC = 01:00 which occurs twice in America/New_York
        fall_back_utc = datetime(2024, 11, 3, 6, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        result = convert_to_timezone(fall_back_utc, "America/New_York")

        assert result is not None
        assert result.astimezone(timezone.utc) == fall_back_utc

    def test_roundtrip_utc_through_dst_transition(self):
        """Converting to local and back to UTC preserves the original value."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone, normalize_to_utc
        original_utc = datetime(2024, 3, 10, 7, 30, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        local = convert_to_timezone(original_utc, "America/New_York")
        back_to_utc = normalize_to_utc(local)

        assert back_to_utc == original_utc

    def test_timestamps_across_dst_boundary_maintain_correct_offset(self):
        """Before/after DST boundary have different offsets but correct UTC."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        before_dst = datetime(2024, 3, 10, 6, 59, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)  # EST (-5)
        after_dst = datetime(2024, 3, 10, 7, 1, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)    # EDT (-4)

        result_before = convert_to_timezone(before_dst, "America/New_York")
        result_after = convert_to_timezone(after_dst, "America/New_York")

        # Offsets differ across the boundary
        assert result_before.utcoffset() != result_after.utcoffset()
        # But both round-trip correctly
        assert result_before.astimezone(timezone.utc) == before_dst
        assert result_after.astimezone(timezone.utc) == after_dst


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. UI Formatter – Rendering in User's Configured Timezone
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestUITimezoneRendering:

    def test_formatter_renders_in_eastern_timezone(self, utc_now, user_eastern):
        """UI displays timestamp in user's Eastern timezone, not UTC."""
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user
        result = format_timestamp_for_user(utc_now, user_eastern)

        assert "10:30" in result, "Eastern time (UTC-4 in summer) should show 10:30"
        assert "UTC" not in result, "UTC label should not appear in user-facing output"

    def test_formatter_renders_in_tokyo_timezone(self, utc_now, user_tokyo):
        """UI displays timestamp in user's Tokyo timezone."""
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user
        result = format_timestamp_for_user(utc_now, user_tokyo)

        assert "23:30" in result, "Tokyo time (UTC+9) should show 23:30"

    def test_formatter_includes_timezone_label(self, utc_now, user_eastern):
        """Rendered timestamp includes the timezone abbreviation or name."""
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user
        result = format_timestamp_for_user(utc_now, user_eastern)

        # Should contain timezone context (e.g., EDT, EST, or America/New_York)
        assert any(label in result for label in ["EDT", "EST", "New_York", "ET"]), (
            f"Expected timezone label in output: '{result}'"
        )

    def test_formatter_falls_back_to_utc_when_no_timezone_configured(
        self, utc_now, user_no_timezone
    ):
        """When user has no configured timezone, UTC is used as fallback."""
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user
        result = format_timestamp_for_user(utc_now, user_no_timezone)

        assert "14:30" in result, "UTC time should show 14:30 as fallback"
        assert "UTC" in result, "Fallback output must indicate UTC explicitly"

    def test_formatter_different_users_same_timestamp_different_output(
        self, utc_now, user_eastern, user_tokyo
    ):
        """Two users in different timezones see different local times."""
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user
        result_eastern = format_timestamp_for_user(utc_now, user_eastern)
        result_tokyo = format_timestamp_for_user(utc_now, user_tokyo)

        assert result_eastern != result_tokyo, (
            "Users in different timezones must see different rendered times"
        )

    def test_formatter_output_is_string(self, utc_now, user_eastern):
        """Formatter returns a string suitable for direct UI rendering."""
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user
        result = format_timestamp_for_user(utc_now, user_eastern)

        assert isinstance(result, str), "Formatted timestamp must be a string"
        assert len(result) > 0, "Formatted timestamp must not be empty"

    def test_formatter_none_timestamp_raises_or_returns_empty(self, user_eastern):
        """A None timestamp is handled gracefully."""
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, TypeError)):
            format_timestamp_for_user(None, user_eastern)

    def test_formatter_accepts_various_date_format_configs(
        self, utc_now, user_eastern
    ):
        """Formatter respects a user-level date format preference."""
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user
        result_iso = format_timestamp_for_user(
            utc_now, user_eastern, date_format="ISO"
        )
        result_us = format_timestamp_for_user(
            utc_now, user_eastern, date_format="US"
        )

        assert result_iso != result_us, "Different format configs must yield different strings"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. API Layer – Response Timestamps
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAPITimestampBehavior:

    def test_api_response_timestamp_is_utc_iso8601(self, utc_now, mock_db_session):
        """API responses serialize timestamps as UTC ISO 8601."""
        from api.endpoints import serialize_event
        from models.event import Event
        event = Event(id="evt-010", name="API Event", created_at=utc_now)

        payload = serialize_event(event)

        assert "created_at" in payload
        ts = payload["created_at"]
        # Must end with Z or +00:00 indicating UTC
        assert ts.endswith("Z") or ts.endswith("+00:00"), (
            f"API timestamp must be UTC ISO 8601, got: '{ts}'"
        )

    def test_api_response_never_contains_local_time(self, utc_now):
        """API payload does not contain local/offset-adjusted time."""
        from api.endpoints import serialize_event
        from models.event import Event
        event = Event(id="evt-011", name="API Event", created_at=utc_now)

        payload = serialize_event(event)
        ts = payload["created_at"]

        # Should NOT contain non-UTC offsets
        assert "-04:00" not in ts
        assert "-05:00" not in ts
        assert "+09:00" not in ts

    def test_api_list_response_all_timestamps_are_utc(self):
        """All timestamps in a list endpoint response are UTC."""
        from api.endpoints import serialize_events
        from models.event import Event
        events = [
            Event(
                id=f"evt-{i}",
                name=f"Event {i}",
                created_at=datetime(2024, 6, 15, i, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
            )
            for i in range(5)
        ]
        payloads = serialize_events(events)

        for payload in payloads:
            ts = payload["created_at"]
            assert ts.endswith("Z") or ts.endswith("+00:00"), (
                f"Non-UTC timestamp found in list response: '{ts}'"
            )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Integration-Style Tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestEndToEndTimezoneFlow:

    def test_timestamp_stored_utc_and_rendered_in_user_timezone(
        self, mock_db_session, user_eastern
    ):
        """
        Full flow: event created → UTC stored → UI renders in user's timezone.
        Verifies the requirement as a single end-to-end scenario.
        """
        from models.event import Event
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user

        # Step 1: Create event (timestamp must be UTC at storage time)
        event = Event.create(name="End-to-End Event")
        assert event.created_at.tzinfo == timezone.utc, (
            "FAIL (storage): Event timestamp is not UTC"
        )

        # Step 2: Simulate retrieval from DB (still UTC)
        stored_ts = event.created_at
        assert stored_ts.utcoffset() == timedelta(0), (
            "FAIL (retrieval): Stored timestamp is not UTC"
        )

        # Step 3: Render for user
        rendered = format_timestamp_for_user(stored_ts, user_eastern)
        assert "UTC" not in rendered or user_eastern.timezone == "UTC", (
            "FAIL (rendering): UTC label shown instead of user's local time"
        )
        assert isinstance(rendered, str) and len(rendered) > 0, (
            "FAIL (rendering): Rendered output is invalid"
        )

    def test_two_events_same_utc_moment_render_correctly_for_each_user(
        self, user_eastern, user_tokyo
    ):
        """Two users see the same event in their respective local times."""
        from models.event import Event
        from ui.formatters import format_timestamp_for_user

        shared_utc = datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        event = Event(id="evt-shared", name="Shared Event", created_at=shared_utc)

        eastern_view = format_timestamp_for_user(event.created_at, user_eastern)
        tokyo_view = format_timestamp_for_user(event.created_at, user_tokyo)

        # Eastern: UTC midnight = previous day 20:00 EDT
        assert "20:00" in eastern_view or "8:00" in eastern_view, (
            f"Eastern rendering unexpected: {eastern_view}"
        )
        # Tokyo: UTC midnight = 09:00 JST
        assert "09:00" in tokyo_view or "9:00" in tokyo_view, (
            f"Tokyo rendering unexpected: {tokyo_view}"
        )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("user_tz,utc_hour,expected_hour", [
        ("America/New_York", 14, 10),   # EDT (UTC-4) in summer
        ("America/Los_Angeles", 14, 7), # PDT (UTC-7) in summer
        ("Europe/London", 14, 15),      # BST (UTC+1) in summer
        ("Europe/Berlin", 14, 16),      # CEST (UTC+2) in summer
        ("Asia/Tokyo", 14, 23),         # JST (UTC+9), always
        ("Asia/Kolkata", 14, 19),       # IST (UTC+5:30) → 19:30
        ("UTC", 14, 14),                # UTC = UTC
    ])
    def test_utc_to_local_hour_parametrized(self, user_tz, utc_hour, expected_hour):
        """Parametrized: validate correct local hour for common timezones."""
        from services.timezone_service import convert_to_timezone
        utc_ts = datetime(2024, 6, 15, utc_hour, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        result = convert_to_timezone(utc_ts, user_tz)

        assert result.hour == expected_hour, (
            f"For {user_tz}: expected hour {expected_hour}, got {result.hour}"
        )
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| `ZoneInfo` over `pytz` | Standard library (Python 3.9+), handles DST correctly by default |
| Naive datetimes rejected explicitly | Prevents ambiguous storage bugs at ingestion boundary |
| Parametrized timezone matrix | Catches regressions when switching TZ libraries |
| DST spring/fall tests isolated | These are the highest-risk edge cases for offset math |
| API serialization tests | The API layer is a common place where UTC discipline breaks down |
| Integration test as narrative | Reads as a requirement trace, useful for audits |

# Pytest Tests for UTC Storage and Timezone Rendering

## Project Structure Assumption
```
project/
├── models/
│   └── event.py          # Data models with timestamp fields
├── services/
│   └── timezone_service.py  # Timezone conversion logic
├── api/
│   └── endpoints.py      # API layer
└── ui/
    └── formatters.py     # UI rendering/formatting
```

---

## Test File

```python
"""
Tests for Requirement: Timestamps stored shall be UTC;
UI shall render in user's configured timezone.

Coverage areas:
1. Storage layer - timestamps are persisted as UTC
2. Ingestion layer - incoming timestamps are normalized to UTC
3. Conversion service - accurate timezone conversion
4. UI formatter - renders in user's configured timezone
5. Edge cases - DST transitions, offsets, invalid zones
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo  # Python 3.9+; use pytz for earlier versions


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def utc_now():
    """A timezone-aware UTC datetime for use across tests."""
    return datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


@pytest.fixture
def naive_utc_now():
    """A naive datetime that represents UTC (no tzinfo attached)."""
    return datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 0)


@pytest.fixture
def user_eastern():
    """Simulated user profile configured for US/Eastern."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-001"
    user.timezone = "America/New_York"
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def user_tokyo():
    """Simulated user profile configured for Asia/Tokyo."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-002"
    user.timezone = "Asia/Tokyo"
    return user


@pytest.fixture

telemetry.raw_response_head:
# Pytest Tests for UTC Storage and Timezone Rendering

## Project Structure Assumption
```
project/
├── models/
│   └── event.py          # Data models with timestamp fields
├── services/
│   └── timezone_service.py  # Timezone conversion logic
├── api/
│   └── endpoints.py      # API layer
└── ui/
    └── formatters.py     # UI rendering/formatting
```

---

## Test File

```python
"""
Tests for Requirement: Timestamps stored shall be UTC;
UI shall render in user's configured timezone.

Coverage areas:
1. Storage layer - timestamps are persisted as UTC
2. Ingestion layer - incoming timestamps are normalized to UTC
3. Conversion service - accurate timezone conversion
4. UI formatter - renders in user's configured timezone
5. Edge cases - DST transitions, offsets, invalid zones
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo  # Python 3.9+; use pytz for earlier versions


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def utc_now():
    """A timezone-aware UTC datetime for use across tests."""
    return datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)


@pytest.fixture
def naive_utc_now():
    """A naive datetime that represents UTC (no tzinfo attached)."""
    return datetime(2024, 6, 15, 14, 30, 0)


@pytest.fixture
def user_eastern():
    """Simulated user profile configured for US/Eastern."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-001"
    user.timezone = "America/New_York"
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def user_tokyo():
    """Simulated user profile configured for Asia/Tokyo."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-002"
    user.timezone = "Asia/Tokyo"
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def user_berlin():
    """Simulated user profile configured for Europe/Berlin."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-003"
    user.timezone = "Europe/Berlin"
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def user_no_timezone():
    """Simulated user with no configured timezone."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-004"
    user.timezone = None
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def mock_db_session():
    """Mock database session for storage assertions."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def sample_event(utc_now):
    """A domain event with a UTC timestamp."""
    from models.event import Event
    return Event(id="evt-001", name="Test Event", created_at=utc_now)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Storage Layer – Timestamps Must Be Persisted as UTC
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestTimestampStoredAsUTC:

    def test_event_created_at_is_stored_with_utc_tzinfo(self, sample_event):
        """Timestamp on a newly created record carries UTC tzinfo."""
        assert sample_event.created_at.tzinfo is not None, (
            "created_at must be timezone-aware"
        )
        assert sample_event.created_at.utcoffset() == timedelta(0), (
            "created_at must have zero UTC offset (i.e., be UTC)"
        )

    def test_event_updated_at_is_stored_as_utc(self):
        """updated_at timestamp reflects UTC when the model is saved."""
        from models.event import Event
        event = Event(id="evt-002", name="Updated Event")
        event.touch()  # simulates an update

        assert event.updated_at.tzinfo == timezone.utc, (
            "updated_at must be UTC after touch()"
        )

    def test_model_factory_always_uses_utc(self):
        """Model factory method never produces a naive timestamp."""
        from models.event import Event
        event = Event.create(name="Factory Event")

        assert event.created_at.tzinfo is not None, (
            "Factory-created events must have timezone-aware timestamps"
        )
        assert event.created_at.ut
